python: 3.13.5 | packaged by Anaconda, Inc. | (main, Jun 12 2025, 16:37:03) [MSC v.1929 64 bit (AMD64)]

results.title: Poincaré / Wirtinger sharpness
results.summary: The first cosine eigenfunction attains equality in the time-domain bound.
details: Used r(t) = cos(pi t / T), the first zero-mean eigenfunction on [0, T].
Verified the temporal mean is exactly zero.
Verified Var_U(r(U)) and (T / pi^2) int_0^T (r'(t))^2 dt simplify to the same closed form.

results.title: Jacobian-velocity theorem on a deterministic path
results.summary: For X_t = t, g = f, and f(x) = cos(pi x / T), the theorem is exact with beta = 1.
details: The deployment path is deterministic, so the expectation operators collapse exactly.
Because g = f, Assumption A3 holds with beta = 1 and |grad g dot Xdot| = |J_f Xdot|.
The Jacobian-velocity right-hand side equals the exact volatility from the lemma-sharp example.
math_blocks: X_t = t,\qquad \dot X_t = 1,\qquad f(x) = g(x) = \cos\!\left(\frac{\pi x}{T}\right)

results.title: Composition case in Remark A3
results.summary: Directional derivatives of g = h o f factor exactly into h'(f) and the score Jacobian term.
details: Used a nontrivial smooth score field f(x1, x2) = x1^2 + x1 x2 + sin(x2).
Used h(z) = atan(z), so g(x1, x2) = atan(f(x1, x2)).
Simplified grad g dot v - h'(f) grad f dot v to zero exactly.

results.title: Rank-1 hazard-score bookkeeping
results.summary: The pointwise algebra behind Proposition 1 is exact; the expectation form follows by averaging these identities.
details: Worked in an orthonormal basis with v = e1, u = e2, Delta mu / Delta = (a, r), and a generic 2 x 2 Jacobian matrix.
Verified s_t^2 = |a|^2 + |r|^2 from the orthogonal block-drift decomposition.
Verified the directional energy splits into aligned, orthogonal, and overlap terms with coefficients c^2, s^2, and 2cs.
math_blocks: \frac{\Delta \mu_t}{\Delta} = a v + r u,\qquad v_t = c v + s u,\qquad v^\top u = 0
\|J_f v_t\|^2 = c^2\|J_f v\|^2 + s^2\|J_f u\|^2 + 2cs\langle J_f v, J_f u\rangle

results.title: Bernoulli cross-entropy derivative bound
results.summary: The soft-target Bernoulli cross-entropy derivative is sigma(z) - q, so its magnitude never exceeds 1.
details: Differentiated the scalar loss h(z) = -q log sigma(z) - (1-q) log(1-sigma(z)) exactly.
Verified the closed form h'(z) = sigma(z) - q symbolically.
Checked the beta = 1 bound over a dense grid in z and q.

results.title: Low-rank corollary inequalities
results.summary: The triangle and Frobenius inequalities used in the low-rank corollary hold across randomized orthonormal decompositions.
details: Generated random orthonormal drift bases V with QR factorization.
Projected residual drift rho into the orthogonal complement of span(V).
Checked the split inequality, the Frobenius inequality, and the combined corollary bound trial by trial.

results.title: Jacobian-velocity theorem with a nontrivial expectation
results.method: dense quadrature on a smooth finite-mixture path
results.summary: A smooth mixture path satisfies the full inequality chain Var <= derivative-energy bound <= Jacobian-velocity bound.
details: Used X_t = t + Z with a three-point discrete random offset Z and deterministic velocity Xdot = 1.
Used f(x) = cos(pi x) and g(x) = atan(f(x)), so A3 holds with beta <= 1.
Integrated the continuous-time quantities on a dense grid to verify the theorem in a genuine expectation setting.

results.title: Synthetic theorem summary bounds
results.summary: Every cached synthetic theorem run satisfies the finite-difference, chain-rule, and Jacobian-velocity bounds.
details: Loaded 8 rows from figures/synthetic_theorem_summary.csv.
Checked volatility <= bound_fd, volatility <= bound_chain, and volatility <= bound_jv wherever those columns exist.
Allowed an absolute tolerance of 5e-5 for cached numerical summaries, since those bounds come from finite grids and Monte Carlo estimates rather than exact algebra.

results.title: Directional comparison bounds
results.summary: Every cached directional-comparison run preserves the volatility upper bounds used in the paper.
details: Loaded 14 rows from figures/synthetic_directional_comparison_summary.csv.
Checked volatility <= bound_fd, volatility <= bound_chain, and volatility <= bound_jv wherever those columns exist.
Allowed an absolute tolerance of 5e-5 for cached numerical summaries, since those bounds come from finite grids and Monte Carlo estimates rather than exact algebra.

results.title: Misspecification bounds
results.summary: Every cached misspecification run preserves the volatility upper bounds under subspace rotation.
details: Loaded 6 rows from figures/synthetic_directional_misspecification_summary.csv.
Checked volatility <= bound_fd, volatility <= bound_chain, and volatility <= bound_jv wherever those columns exist.
Allowed an absolute tolerance of 5e-5 for cached numerical summaries, since those bounds come from finite grids and Monte Carlo estimates rather than exact algebra.
